title: ledum palustre f. decumbens
intro: Ledum palustre f. decumbens, also known as Labrador tea, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the heath family, Ericaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating colds, fever, and respiratory issues due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an antiseptic. Ledum palustre f. decumbens can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
